title: Line2D Object
description: Transient 2D line. A transient line is not displayed or saved in a document. Transient 2D lines are used as a wrapper to work with raw 2D line information. They are created statically using the create method of the Line2D class.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: copy
methods.description: Creates and returns a copy of this line object.

methods.name: getData
methods.description: Gets all of the data defining the line segment.

methods.name: set
methods.description: Sets all of the data defining the line segment.

properties.name: asNurbsCurve
properties.description: Returns a NURBS curve that is geometrically identical to the line.

properties.name: curveType
properties.description: Returns the type of geometry this curve represents.

properties.name: endPoint
properties.description: Gets and sets the end point of the line.

properties.name: evaluator
properties.description: Returns an evaluator object that lets you perform additional evaluations on the curve.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: startPoint
properties.description: Gets and sets the start point of the line.

It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

classType.title: Line2D.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

copy.title: Line2D.copy Method
copy.description: Creates and returns a copy of this line object.
copy.return_description: Returns an independent copy of this line object.

create.title: Line2D.create Method
create.return_description: Returns the new Line2D object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: startPoint
parameters.description: The start point of the line

parameters.name: endPoint
parameters.description: The end point of the line

getData.title: Line2D.getData Method
getData.description: Gets all of the data defining the line segment.

parameters.name: startPoint
parameters.description: The output start point of the line.

parameters.name: endPoint
parameters.description: The output end point of the line.

set.title: Line2D.set Method
set.description: Sets all of the data defining the line segment.
set.return_description: Returns true if redefining the line is successful

parameters.name: startPoint
parameters.description: The start point of the line

parameters.name: endPoint
parameters.description: The end point of the line

transformBy.title: Line2D.transformBy Method
transformBy.return_description: Return true if the transform was successful.

parameters.name: matrix
parameters.description: A 2D matrix that defines the transform to apply to the curve.
